name: Accessibility
description: Designing digital systems, content, and standards so they are usable by people with disabilities and across diverse contexts.

name: AI
description: Artificial intelligence, including open source AI, ethical and transparent systems, and AI's impact on work and the public good.

name: Digital Public Infrastructure
description: Shared, interoperable building blocks such as identity, payments, and data exchange that underpin inclusive digital societies.

name: Digital Public Goods
description: Open source software, data, AI models, standards, and content that advance the Sustainable Development Goals.

name: Open Standards
description: Openly developed specifications that enable interoperability and portability across systems and borders.

name: Open Source
description: Software, hardware, and practices developed and shared under open licenses with collaborative governance.

name: Procurement
description: Public-sector purchasing models and policies that favour reuse, openness, and avoidance of vendor lock-in.

name: Climate
description: Open technologies and data addressing climate resilience, disaster preparedness, and environmental risk.

name: Sustainability
description: Long-term maintenance, funding, and stewardship of open source software and digital infrastructure.

name: Government
description: Public-sector adoption of open source, including national strategies, OSPOs, and digital transformation.

name: Health
description: Open source and digital public goods supporting health information systems and humanitarian response.

name: Education
description: Open knowledge, skills development, capacity building, and academic engagement with open source.

name: Security
description: Software supply-chain security, safeguards, data protection, and trust in digital systems.

name: Licensing
description: Open source licenses, definitions, and the legal frameworks governing reuse and contribution.

name: Community
description: Contributor health, recognition, onboarding, and the human practices that sustain open collaboration.

name: Funding
description: Financing models for open source maintenance, including public funds and philanthropic support.

name: OSPO
description: Open Source Program Offices that coordinate open source strategy, compliance, and culture in institutions.

name: Interoperability
description: The ability of systems, data, and services to work together across organisational and national boundaries.

name: Digital Sovereignty
description: Reducing dependency and retaining control over critical digital infrastructure, data, and technology choices.
